titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Hardie Gramatky Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday February 9, 2020 at 04:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection documents the life and career of illustrator, author, and painter, Hardie Gramatky (1907-1979). Materials in the collection include correspondence, photographs, personal papers, and materials related to Gramatky's career as a children's book author and illustrator.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hardie Gramatky (1907-1979) was an American illustrator, painter, and author. He was best known for writing and illustrating children's books, the most famous of which was Little Toot . As a painter, Gramatky gained recognition as a watercolorist, and his works are part of the permanent collections of many American art museums.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the life and career of illustrator, author, and painter, Hardie Gramatky. Materials in the collection include correspondence, photographs, personal papers, and materials related to Gramatky's career as a children's book author and illustrator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Hardie Gramatky Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hardie Gramatky Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this collection has been organized by acquisition reflecting the fact that the collection has been acquired in increments over time. Researchers should note that material within each acquisition overlaps with/or relates to material found in other acquisitions. Researchers should also note that similar material can be arranged differently in each acquisition, depending on how the material was organized when it was received by the library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Linda Gramatky Smith on the Jockey Hollow Fund, 2015. Gift of Linda Gramatky Smith, 2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 24, 57-59, 93 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 61 (born digital material): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence is grouped by year, but not arranged chronologically within years.

did.unittitle: Business/personal correspondence, condolensce letters addressed to Dorothea Gramatky, lists of contributions in honor of Gramatky's death
Condolensce letters addressed to Dorothea Gramatky, children's letters from school author talks
Children's letters from school author talks

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Correspondence is arranged alphabetically by correspondant, then chronologically.

did.unittitle: Artwork by, and photographs of, Gramatky ancestors (11x14)
Autograph book and other printed material owned by, and related to, Gramatky family
Materials related to Gramatky family genealogy, loose photographs from 1920s photo album

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: A package of loose photographs from early family photo album were removed and placed in this box 12.

did.unittitle: Materials relating to Hardie Gramatky's education, special letters, biographical data, obits and memorial service ephemera

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Loose photographs in self-contained package removed from album and placed in Box 14.

did.unittitle: "Adventures and Travels of the Dauntless Three," scrapbook
Address books, business cards, calendars, diaries, ledgers, notebooks, and passports
Address books, business card, calendars, diaries, ledgers, passports, and notebooks
Address books, calendars, diaries, ledgers, passports, and notebooks, materials related to trip to Russia and Georgia
Notes, speeches, New York University course notes, author publicity files
TV appearance in Jacksonville, Florida film reel

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material in cold storage. This item may not be requested. Reference copies may be available: consult Access Services. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Enclosed letter with Jacksonville television appearance reel and Barton Film Company packing slip
Trip to Europe: France England, Ireland scrapbook
Photographs of Hardie, Dorothea, and Linda Gramatky
Oversized photographs of Hardie, Dorothea, and their dog, Punkie, Hardie's first studio and murals

Materials relating to Hardie Gramatky's art and writing career
Hardie early art career scrapbook featuring Little Toot
"Noted Artists Do Portraits of Wounded Fighting Men," Birmingham General Hospital, Van Nuys California scrapbook
Little Toot on the Thames scrapbook
Little Toot on the Grand Canal scrapbook
Little Toot on the Mississippi scrapbook
Art and writing career scrapbook featuring Bosti school visit
Tearsheets, proofs, sketches, book ideas, and show materials
Ahambra show material, Junior Time covers
Children's book tearsheets, Loopy folded and gathered proof
Gramatky mounted tearsheets, Reader's Digest covers, exhibition catalogs
Extras, including stories never heard before
Extras, including stories never heard before
Tearsheets, Little Toot bound proofs with notes by Dorothea Gramatky
Children's book tearsheets, Little Toot sketches, bookmark proofs
Little Toot proofs and posters, Nikos and the Sea God color progressive proofs
Little Toot and the Lochness Monsters dummy and proofs
Children's book ideas: original sketch ideas for Little Toot , "French dog story," and Creeper
Audiovisual material related to Little Toot

did.unittitle: Notes, awards, publicity, and other artwork related materials
Early artwork, school talk materials, Alahambra reunion
Photographs and early examples of Hardie's work
Art awards, children's literature workshop and book fair programs
Art notes, publicity, sketches, Air Force art materials
Art notes, publicity, sketches, Air Force art materials
Art awards, artwork of others, Little Toot painting, Little Toot on the Grand Canal party guest log
Materials removed from Gramatky printed materials
Letters and artwork from children from school talks, Linda Gramatky early artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is arranged by year. A few letters from 1979, addressed to Hardie Gramatky, are enclosed.

did.unittitle: Materials related to magazine and children's book illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files contain correspondence, agreements, and brochures from institions and corporations with information or materials created by, or related to, Hardie Gramatky's works.

did.unittitle: Children's letters and artwork, Gramatky-designed Christmas cards and magazine tear sheets
Little Toot files and book proofs after 1979
Little Toot interior mechanics and spreads, proofs, folded and gathered proof
Correspondence, tax returns, notebooks, slides, travel logs, photographs
Photograph of Hardie, Dorothea, and Linda, and Little Toot Leukemia Art Auction poster
